task: I need to reply to a message about a group project, but I’m not sure how much detail to include. Can you help me draft a short response that gets the point across without turning it into a whole thing?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — keep it short, neutral, and specific.

You can send:

**“Hey, just to clarify: [briefly state what happened]. I’ve already handled my part, and I need you to [specific thing] by [time/date]. Please confirm.”**

If you want it a little firmer:

**“Just to be clear: [briefly state what happened]. I’ve taken care of my part. I need you to [specific thing] by [time/date], and then confirm that’s done.”**

If you want, paste the situation and I’ll turn it into an actual message you can send.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, but that’s still kind of generic. I need it to sound short and not dramatic, not like some template with brackets in it. Give me an actual message I could send for a group project where I’m basically just asking them to do their part and stop dragging it out.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a real message you could send:

**“Hey, I’ve finished my part of the project. Please get your section done and sent over by [time/date] so we can wrap this up.”**

If you want, I can make it:
- a little firmer
- more casual
- or more polite without sounding soft

user_simulator.text: Close, but I need it even less template-y. That still sounds kind of scripted. Give me 2-3 actual text versions, short, like something a normal student would send, not all polite and polished.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here are a few short, more natural ones:

1. **“I’m done with my part. Send yours when it’s ready so we can finish this.”**

2. **“I finished mine already. Just need your part so we can turn it in.”**

3. **“My section’s done. Please get yours in so this doesn’t keep dragging out.”**

If you want, I can make them **more blunt** or **more casual**.

user_simulator.text:
1. “I’m done with my part. Send yours when you’re done so we can finish this.”

2. “My part’s finished. Just need yours so we can turn it in.”

3. “I already did mine. Send your section over so this doesn’t keep dragging.”
